body:
Added the Viewport part to the Menu, allowing for animated content transitions when using detached triggers.

https://deploy-preview-4060--base-ui.netlify.app/react/components/menu#animating-the-menu
https://deploy-preview-4060--base-ui.netlify.app/experiments/menu/triggers

| --- _To edit notification comments on pull requests, go to your [Netlify project configuration](https://app.netlify.com/projects/base-ui/configuration/notifications#deploy-notifications)._

Greptile Overview

Greptile Summary

Adds `Menu.Viewport` component to enable animated content transitions when using detached triggers. The viewport wraps menu content and handles morphing between different content states as users switch between triggers. **Key changes:** - New `MenuViewport` component with transition state management - Enhanced `usePopupViewport` hook with `ReactDOM.flushSync` for better animation timing - `MenuPositioner` now tracks trigger changes and applies `adaptiveOrigin` middleware when viewport is present - Comprehensive test coverage for viewport transitions and morphing behaviors - Documentation and demos showing position, size, and content animations **Minor issue:** - Documentation comment in `MenuViewportDataAttributes.ts:24` missing `'trigger-change'` type value

Confidence Score: 5/5

- This PR is safe to merge with minimal risk - The implementation follows repository guidelines (uses `useIsoLayoutEffect`, `useStableCallback`, proper hook patterns), includes comprehensive tests, and the only issue found is a minor documentation comment inconsistency that doesn't affect runtime behavior - No files require special attention

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/react/src/menu/viewport/MenuViewport.tsx | New MenuViewport component implementation - clean, follows repository guidelines | | packages/react/src/menu/viewport/MenuViewportDataAttributes.ts | Data attributes enum with documentation comment missing `trigger-change` type | | packages/react/src/utils/usePopupViewport.tsx | Improved viewport hook using ReactDOM.flushSync and proper null checks | | packages/react/src/menu/positioner/MenuPositioner.tsx | Added viewport support with adaptiveOrigin and trigger change tracking | | docs/reference/generated/menu-viewport.json | API reference documentation missing `trigger-change` in data-instant type | Last reviewed commit: 57140e8

<!-- greptile-status -->
Automatic reviews are disabled for this repository.

You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).
To continue using code reviews, you can upgrade your account or add credits to your account and enable them for code reviews in your [settings](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/code-review).

## Codex Review

### Overview
This PR adds `Menu.Viewport` so one detached-trigger menu can animate its content, size, and position while reusing the same popup. It also hooks Menu into the shared popup viewport machinery and adds the positioner/state plumbing needed for those transitions.

### Findings (None)
No blocking issues found in this patch.

### Confidence: 4/5
High confidence based on a full pass over the current branch diff, a focused sweep of the new viewport/control-flow paths, targeted JSDOM and Chromium verification of the changed Menu tests, and a small Chromium DOM benchmark for the new transition work. Residual risk is limited to highly app-specific styling or accessibility edge cases around cloned transition content.

### Notes
- I am not re-raising the earlier duplicate-ID concern as a blocker. The current branch forces the live subtree to remount when the active trigger changes, and again if the payload arrives a render later, via `usePopupContentKey` in `packages/react/src/utils/usePopupViewport.tsx:88-90` and `packages/react/src/utils/usePopupViewport.tsx:339-367`. That means the active subtree gets fresh generated IDs instead of reusing the exiting subtree's IDs. I also tried a focused Chromium repro with grouped menu labels and did not reproduce duplicate current/previous ID collisions during the transition window.
- The size tradeoff is still real but bounded. The bundle checker on this PR reports `+1.09KB parsed / +441B gzip` versus `master`, which mostly looks like the cost of exposing `Menu.Viewport` and wiring Menu into the shared viewport path (`packages/react/src/menu/viewport/MenuViewport.tsx`, `packages/react/src/menu/positioner/MenuPositioner.tsx`, `packages/react/src/menu/store/MenuStore.ts`, and `packages/react/src/utils/usePopupViewport.tsx`).
- Focused Chromium DOM micro-check for representative detached-trigger switches (`10` samples, `300` switches per sample):

| Ref | Mean (ms) | p50 (ms) | p95 (ms) | Sample size |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| `master` baseline | 3.18 | 3.10 | 3.80 | 10 |
| PR head | 4.67 | 4.50 | 4.90 | 10 |
| PR head + cloned-subtree sanitization alternative | 6.67 | 6.40 | 7.00 | 10 |

  On this synthetic switch path, the current implementation adds about `1.49ms / 300` switches relative to `master` (roughly `5µs` per switch), while the reverted cloned-subtree sanitization approach would add roughly another `2.00ms / 300` switches on top of the current path. That lines up with the code tradeoff here: the viewport feature is not free, but the remaining transient clone window is short-lived and the heavier sanitization fix appears more expensive in both bytes and per-switch DOM work.
- Verified with `pnpm test:jsdom MenuViewport --no-watch`, `pnpm test:chromium MenuViewport --no-watch`, and `pnpm test:chromium MenuRoot.detached-triggers --no-watch`; all passed.

You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).
To continue using code reviews, you can upgrade your account or add credits to your account and enable them for code reviews in your [settings](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/code-review).

This might be slightly problematic if the contents are styled based on their IDs, so there is a tradeoff.
Traversing all the cloned nodes and modifying their attributes would impact performance, so I'm ignoring it for now.

reviews.body:
<sub>19 files reviewed, 1 comment</sub>

<sub>[Edit Code Review Agent Settings](https://app.greptile.com/review/github) | [Greptile](https://greptile.com?utm_source=greptile_expert&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=code_reviews)</sub>

## Pull request overview This pull request implements the `Menu.Viewport` component to enable animated content transitions when using detached triggers with the Menu component. This mirrors the existing Viewport implementations in Popover, PreviewCard, and Tooltip components, providing a consistent API across the library. **Changes:** - Added `Menu.Viewport` component for content transition animations with detached triggers - Enhanced `usePopupViewport` hook with `ReactDOM.flushSync` for more reliable animation timing - Added `trigger-change` instant type to Menu store for transition behavior control - Integrated `adaptiveOrigin` middleware conditionally based on viewport presence ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 18 out of 19 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `packages/react/src/utils/usePopupViewport.tsx` | Improved animation timing with ReactDOM.flushSync and defensive null handling for CSS variables | | `packages/react/src/menu/viewport/MenuViewport.tsx` | New viewport component implementation following established patterns | | `packages/react/src/menu/viewport/MenuViewportDataAttributes.ts` | Data attribute enum definitions for viewport states | | `packages/react/src/menu/viewport/MenuViewportCssVars.ts` | CSS variable enum definitions for popup dimensions | | `packages/react/src/menu/viewport/MenuViewport.test.tsx` | Comprehensive test suite covering transitions and direction calculations | | `packages/react/src/menu/store/MenuStore.ts` | Added `hasViewport` state and `trigger-change` instant type | | `packages/react/src/menu/root/MenuRoot.detached-triggers.test.tsx` | Added regression test for inline scale styles | | `packages/react/src/menu/positioner/MenuPositioner.tsx` | Integrated adaptiveOrigin middleware and trigger change detection | | `packages/react/src/menu/popup/MenuPopup.tsx` | Added popup element tracking callback and updated instant type | | `packages/react/src/menu/index.ts` | Added Viewport type exports | | `packages/react/src/menu/index.parts.ts` | Added Viewport component export | | `docs/src/app/(private)/experiments/menu/triggers.tsx` | Added Viewport to experimental demo | | `docs/src/app/(docs)/react/components/page.mdx` | Updated component structure documentation | | `docs/src/app/(docs)/react/components/menu/page.mdx` | Added comprehensive documentation for animating menus | | `docs/src/app/(docs)/react/components/menu/demos/detached-triggers-full/*` | New demo implementations in Tailwind and CSS modules | | `docs/reference/generated/menu-viewport.json` | Generated API reference documentation |
